content: agent1: implementing 'Sync Features dictionaries' - adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class in src/datasets/features/features.py to sync _column_requires_decoding dict on __setitem__/__delitem__/update/etc. Will only touch that file.

agent1: Already implemented keep_features_dicts_synced decorator and applied it to __setitem__, __delitem__, update, setdefault, pop, popitem, clear in Features (src/datasets/features/features.py). For update() I currently just wrote: def update(self, *args, **kwargs): super().update(*args, **kwargs) -- wrapped with @keep_features_dicts_synced. Please layer your deep-merge body logic inside that same method (keep the @keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on top, just change the body from 'super().update(*args, **kwargs)' to your merge logic). Ping me before you save so we don't clobber each other on that one method - happy for you to edit it directly since decorator + sync logic is separate from merge logic.

agent1: Thanks - looks like we're actually on separate copies of the repo (my working copy doesn't show your _update_feature changes), so there's no literal file clobbering risk. For my patch, update() is just 'def update(self, *args, **kwargs): super().update(*args, **kwargs)' wrapped with @keep_features_dicts_synced - matches dict.update semantics. If your final patch keeps the decorator on update() and routes single-key assigns through __setitem__ (or otherwise recomputes _column_requires_decoding), it'll compose fine when merged.
